metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit precondition to the Tests AC that the live check requires S1 (Autosubmit label) to be merged and `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` to return 200 before executing — preventing a vacuous pass on an empty selection queue.
findings.finding: The live-check AC (Tests bullet) exercises `rebase:chain + rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` against the live Gerrit instance, which requires the `Autosubmit` label to exist in `project.config`. The `Autosubmit` label is absent from the current live `infra/gerrit/project.config` (only `LLM-Review` and `Verified` are defined) and S1 (sterilized-papery-horse) is still open. A `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` query against a Gerrit instance without the label returns zero results or an error — the live check's selection step will see an empty queue and the test will silently pass vacuously. Evidence class: Contradicted.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests: 'a live check exercises rebase:chain + rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Tests AC, add: 'Precondition: S1 (sterilized-papery-horse) must be merged and `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` must return HTTP 200 before this live check is executed.' Alternatively, add a guard at the top of the live-check script that fails fast if the label is absent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete merge-detection predicate to the Rebase-routing AC (e.g., 'detect a merge change by inspecting `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/commit`; a change is a merge if `len(commit.parents) > 1`') so the routing condition is fully specified without executor inference.
findings.finding: The rebase-routing AC specifies that 'a `--no-ff` merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase' but never specifies HOW the code detects that a change is a merge commit. `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` is a RebaseInput body field (what to send), not a detection predicate (how to know). An executor must independently discover and apply a detection method — most naturally checking `len(revision.parents) > 1` from the commit-info object — but this is fully absent from the plan, making the routing branch mechanically underspecified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-routing bullet: 'a `--no-ff` merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase (first-parent-only MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE), NEVER rebase:chain'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Rebase-routing AC: 'Detect a merge change by calling `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/commit` and checking `len(commit_info["parents"]) > 1`; the merge branch is entered only when this predicate is true. Use `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` in the RebaseInput body for that branch.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **Rebase-routing AC:** Add the explicit merge-detection rule — `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/commit → len(parents) >= 2 → is_merge` — co-located in the AC bullet that defines the merge routing branch, so it is unambiguous without cross-referencing voter.py.
findings.finding: The rebase-routing decision rule for 'merge change' detection is never specified. The AC states 'a --no-ff merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase (first-parent-only MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE)' but gives no API call or field that determines whether a change IS a merge commit before routing. MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE is the Gerrit change-kind label applied AFTER a rebase operation; it is not a pre-routing signal. An implementer of loop.py must independently discover the correct detection mechanism (GET /revisions/current/commit → len(parents) >= 2, as used in voter.py), with no spec-level authority.
findings.location: AC — Rebase-routing bullet: 'a `--no-ff` merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase (first-parent-only MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the merge-routing AC bullet: 'Detect a merge change via `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/commit`; if `len(CommitInfo.parents) >= 2` the change is a merge and ALWAYS routes to `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` — NEVER `rebase:chain`. This check precedes the RelatedChanges chain-membership check.'

findings.finding: The plan never specifies HOW to detect a merge change for routing purposes. MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE is a RebaseInput field (sent in the body), not a detection mechanism. An executor must independently decide which Gerrit API field or heuristic identifies a change as a merge commit (e.g., checking `parents` count on the revision object, a dedicated Gerrit option like `MERGE_LIST`, or something else).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-routing bullet: 'a `--no-ff` merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Rebase-routing AC: 'Detect a merge change by inspecting the `parents` array on the current revision object; a change is a merge if `len(revision.parents) > 1`.'

findings.finding: The rebase-routing AC specifies that a '--no-ff merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase (first-parent-only MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE)' but MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE is a RebaseInput body field (what to send in the POST), not a detection signal. The plan never specifies how loop.py detects that a given change IS a merge commit — e.g. by checking that the revision's `parents` array has length > 1 via GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/commit. Without the detection step, an implementer has no specification for the routing branch condition, and the existing gerrit_client.py's get_commit() / parents-length pattern is not referenced.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-routing bullet: 'a `--no-ff` merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase (first-parent-only `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`), NEVER `rebase:chain`'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Rebase-routing AC bullet: 'Detect a merge change via `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/commit`: if `len(commit.parents) > 1` the change is a merge and must use single `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `{"strategy": "MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE"}` in the body. This reuses `gerrit_client.get_commit()`'s existing pattern.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **AC — Selection + Rebase-routing:** Add a fallback/error-handling rule for each Gerrit sub-step: specify what loop.py does when GET /changes, GET /related, or POST /rebase(/:chain) returns an error or non-2xx (e.g., log + skip + sleep, or escalate to S3 hand-back).
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for any failed Gerrit sub-step in the selection or rebase-routing path. The AC is entirely happy-path: if `GET /changes` returns an error or empty set, if `GET /related` fails, or if `POST /rebase` / `POST /rebase:chain` returns a 409 (conflict) or other non-2xx, the plan specifies no behavior. This is a structural gap for an executor implementing loop.py.
findings.location: AC — all five AC bullets (Selection, Rebase-routing, rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader, Gerrit helper, Tests)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a routing rule for the mixed-chain case (a relation chain containing at least one merge member) — e.g., 'if any chain member is a merge commit, route each member individually via POST /changes/{id}/rebase rather than rebase:chain' — so the three routing branches are exhaustive.
findings.finding: The rebase-routing logic covers three disjoint branches (merge-change → POST rebase; >1-member non-merge chain → POST rebase:chain; single non-merge → POST rebase) but leaves the mixed-chain case unaddressed: a Gerrit relation chain can contain both merge and non-merge members (e.g., a feature-branch merge change stacked on top of ordinary changes). In that scenario the RelatedChanges API returns >1 member, but rebase:chain is explicitly forbidden for merge members. The plan provides no routing rule for this combination.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-routing bullet: '>1-member non-merge chain rebases with rebase:chain … a `--no-ff` merge change ALWAYS uses POST rebase … a single non-merge change uses POST rebase'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **Rebase-routing AC:** Clarify the precedence ordering: state explicitly that the merge-detection check (parents >= 2) runs FIRST and overrides the chain-membership check, so any merge commit — even one inside a relation chain — always routes to single `POST /rebase`.
findings.finding: The merge-routing rule and the chain-routing rule interact but are specified in isolation, creating an instruction-locality gap. A change that is both a merge commit AND a member of a relation chain (a --no-ff merge change in a stack) would satisfy both the '>1-member' chain check and the merge-detection check. The plan never states the precedence — which check runs first, or whether a merge-commit chain member is treated as single (merge) rather than chain.
findings.location: AC — Rebase-routing bullet (two sub-conditions: merge check and chain check)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a precedence note: 'Merge detection takes priority: if the selected change (or any chain member being routed) has >= 2 parents, route to single POST /rebase regardless of chain membership.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit retry/backoff (e.g., exponential with jitter, max 3 attempts) and timeout to every Gerrit HTTP call in gerrit.py, and surface non-retriable errors (conflict, permanent 4xx) as logged, observable failures rather than silent drops or unhandled exceptions.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no retry/backoff, timeout, or circuit-breaker for any of the Gerrit HTTP calls (GET changes, GET related, POST rebase, POST rebase:chain, POST submit), and no error handling when those calls fail (network error, 5xx, 409 conflict on rebase). Error states appear to be swallowed or crash the loop.
findings.location: Files: infra/autolander/gerrit.py; AC: Gerrit helper + rebase-routing bullets
findings.suggested_fix: In gerrit.py, wrap each HTTP call in a helper that retries on 5xx/network errors with exponential backoff (e.g., 1s, 2s, 4s), applies a per-call timeout (e.g., 30s), and raises a typed exception (GerritError) for non-retriable responses. In loop.py, catch GerritError and log it with the change ID + error code before continuing to the next cycle.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the transport posture for `infra/autolander/gerrit.py`: specify whether the Gerrit base URL must be HTTPS (matching the canonical URL used by `gerrit_client.py`) or, if the internal Docker bridge http://gerrit:8080 is used, explicitly state that rationale and note that the bot credential is confined to the private host network.
findings.finding: The plan does not declare whether the autolander Gerrit helper will use the canonical HTTPS base URL or the internal Docker bridge URL (http://gerrit:8080). Basic auth credentials (the SSM-backed HTTP password) are base64-encoded in the Authorization header; sending them over a plaintext http:// path — even on a private Docker bridge — is an undeclared transport posture that could expose the token to any co-tenant process with network visibility on the same host.
findings.location: Gerrit helper AC / Files: `infra/autolander/gerrit.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or config note specifying that `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_URL` must be set to the canonical HTTPS URL (e.g. `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com`) — or alternatively note that the internal Docker network http://gerrit:8080 is deliberately used and the Basic auth token is acceptable on a private loopback-equivalent network, citing the single-tenant host boundary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify at minimum structured logging (change ID, operation, outcome) for every Gerrit call outcome and a queue-depth / error-count metric so operators can detect and alert on autolander failures.
findings.finding: No observability (logging, metrics, or alerting) is specified for any failure point: failed rebase, failed submit, conflict on rebase:chain, or Gerrit unavailability. Operators cannot detect or diagnose autolander failures.
findings.location: Files: infra/autolander/loop.py, infra/autolander/gerrit.py; AC section overall

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test cases for failure/error/empty paths in the Gerrit helper and selection logic: HTTP 4xx/5xx on rebase, 409 conflict, network timeout, and empty selection-query result — and specify what observable behavior (exception type, logged warning, skip-and-continue) the loop guarantees for each.
findings.finding: The test plan specifies only happy-path unit tests (FIFO ordering, routing) and one live happy-path scenario (`rebase:chain` success). No test work is specified for failure, error, or degenerate paths in the new Gerrit helper or selection logic: HTTP 409 conflict on rebase, 4xx/5xx from any Gerrit endpoint, network timeout in the stdlib helper, or an empty/zero-result change list from the selection query. These are all reachable in production and the selection+routing loop would need to handle them gracefully.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet
findings.suggested_fix: In the Tests AC, add: 'Unit tests also cover: (a) HTTP 409 conflict on rebase — loop logs and skips the change; (b) HTTP 5xx from any endpoint — helper raises a typed exception; (c) selection query returning zero results — loop idles without error; (d) urllib timeout — helper surfaces a TimeoutError to the loop.' Define the expected observable outcome for each so tests are behavioral rather than implementation-coupled.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a live-instance acceptance criterion for the merge-change rebase path: submit a synthetic `--no-ff` merge change to the test Gerrit instance, exercise the routing, and assert the rebase used `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` (e.g., verify via the resulting commit parentage or the Gerrit audit log).
findings.finding: The merge-change rebase path (`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`) is tested ONLY via recorded Gerrit JSON unit tests, with no live-instance validation. The `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` parameter is a distinct, instance-specific Gerrit behavior; a mis-named or unsupported field would cause the recorded-JSON unit test to pass while the live call silently falls back to a full-parent rebase (or errors). The live check covers only the `rebase:chain` path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-routing bullet + Tests bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the live-check AC: 'A live check also exercises the merge-change path: a synthetic --no-ff merge change is routed to POST /changes/{id}/rebase with MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE=true, and the resulting patchset is asserted to have exactly one parent (first-parent-only rebase).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Mark `infra/autolander/loop.py` and `infra/autolander/gerrit.py` as NEW files (not edits) in the Files section, and note that the `infra/autolander/` directory itself must be created.
findings.finding: Both `infra/autolander/loop.py` and `infra/autolander/gerrit.py` are listed as FILES without noting they are NEW creations — the directory `infra/autolander/` does not exist in the repo (confirmed: directory absent; parent ticket S2's own plan-review manifest notes 'dep absent infra/autolander/loop.py'). Labeling them as if they are edit targets of existing files is a scope-classification error; they are out-of-scope CREATION targets per the rubric, and should be called out explicitly as new files.
findings.location: ## Files section — `infra/autolander/loop.py` and `infra/autolander/gerrit.py`
findings.suggested_fix: In the Files section, prefix both entries with 'NEW:' or add a note '(create; directory does not yet exist)' to make the creation intent unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in the Files section that `infra/autolander/loop.py` is also edited by S2b and S2c, and state the intended authoring sequence or partitioning (e.g., 'S2a creates the skeleton; S2b and S2c add to it in order').
findings.finding: `infra/autolander/loop.py` is also named as an edit target by sibling tasks S2b (`a9e3-580c-409c-4eee`) and S2c (`1ccf-ca1e-f832-452e`). This plan does not acknowledge the shared-file situation or specify a merge/ordering strategy, which is a missing consumer/coordination enumeration.
findings.location: ## Files section — `infra/autolander/loop.py`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` exists in Gerrit 3.14.1 by citing the specific Gerrit release note or REST API doc page (e.g., gerrit-review.googlesource.com/Documentation/rest-api-changes.html for 3.14), and add a live smoke-probe to the e2e test that confirms a 200/204 (not 404/405) before committing the routing path.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` is available on this Gerrit 3.14.1 instance with the note 'confirmed on this instance's docs', but no repo artifact (docs/, infra/gerrit/, e2e scripts, or plugin list) actually demonstrates or verifies this endpoint exists in 3.14.1. The rebase:chain endpoint was introduced in later Gerrit releases and its availability on 3.14.x should be verified against actual API docs or a live probe before the plan commits to it.
findings.location: AC — Rebase-routing bullet: 'a >1-member non-merge chain rebases with POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain (confirmed on this instance's docs)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` is a documented, accepted field for `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` in Gerrit 3.14.1 (cite the API doc section), and add an explicit live-test assertion that inspects the rebased patchset's `uploader._account_id` via `GET /changes/{id}?o=DETAILED_ACCOUNTS` — not just the commit trailers — to verify the field was honored by the chain endpoint.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` applies to both `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` and (by implication) `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain`, but the repo contains no evidence that this field is accepted by the `rebase:chain` endpoint. The existing `gerrit_client.py` never uses this field, and Gerrit's `RebaseInput` documentation for chain-rebase may differ from single-change rebase.
findings.location: AC — third bullet: '`RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves the uploader + DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Provide a concrete, citable verification artifact for `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` on the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance (e.g. a curl invocation against the instance's Swagger/API docs URL, a successful live test output, or a Gerrit changelog reference) — replace the self-asserted 'confirmed on this instance's docs' with a linked proof.
findings.finding: `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` is asserted as 'confirmed on this instance's docs' but no linked doc artifact, URL, or captured API response is cited in the plan or repo. No existing repo code or test fixture exercises this endpoint. Evidence class: Unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-routing: 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain (confirmed on this instance's docs; linear ancestry only)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a verify_command or linked doc reference: e.g. `curl -s -u $BOT /a/Documentation/rest-api-changes.html | grep -o 'rebase:chain'` returning a hit, or a note referencing the Gerrit 3.x REST API docs URL with the exact path. Alternatively, add a targeted SPIKE AC: 'before implementation, POST /changes/{test-change-id}/rebase:chain against the live instance and record the HTTP status + response body as a fixture.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before committing to `RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true`, verify (via a live curl against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance or the instance's REST API docs) that (a) the field is accepted in the rebase POST body on this version, and (b) the required bot capability is grantable as a global capability entry — and cite the result in the plan.
findings.finding: `RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` requires the bot account to hold the `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` server-level global capability in Gerrit. Whether this is a valid grantable capability on Gerrit 3.14.1 (vs. a project.config submit-strategy flag) is disputed in sibling plan reviews for S5 and is Unverified in this plan. If the capability mechanism is wrong, the field in the POST body will be silently ignored or return 403, and every auto-rebased patchset will record the bot as uploader — breaking the DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer invariant this AC exists to protect.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves the uploader + DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete Gerrit JSON field/condition used to detect a merge commit (e.g., `len(revision_obj['commit']['parents']) > 1`) in the rebase-routing AC so the executor can implement and fixture-test the detection without ambiguity.
findings.finding: The plan references 'a --no-ff merge change' and 'MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE' as the routing branch that must always use `POST /changes/{id}/rebase`, but never specifies HOW to detect a merge commit from Gerrit's JSON response. An executor writing `loop.py` has no stated criterion (e.g., checking `len(revision['commit']['parents']) > 1`) and must guess the detection logic.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-routing bullet: 'a --no-ff merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase (first-parent-only MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE), NEVER rebase:chain'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the rebase-routing AC: 'A change is classified as a merge commit when its current revision's `commit.parents` array has length > 1 (as returned by `GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION&o=CURRENT_COMMIT`).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the SSM parameter path for RebarBotNava's HTTP password (e.g., `/rebar/gerrit/rebarbotnava/http_password`) so `gerrit.py` can be written without consulting the S5 ticket.
findings.finding: The Gerrit helper must authenticate as RebarBotNava via an SSM-backed HTTP password 'provisioned by S5', but the plan does not state the SSM parameter name/path. An executor writing `gerrit.py` must either guess the convention or stop and ask, which conflicts with E2's executability requirement.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Gerrit helper bullet: 'the bot authenticates as RebarBotNava via its SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete invocation command or named test target for the live check (e.g., `pytest -m live infra/autolander/tests/test_live_rebase.py`) to the Tests AC so it can be independently re-run to verify completion.
findings.finding: The live check AC names what to assert (uploader unchanged, trailers preserved) but specifies no invocation command or test target path. Without a named pytest marker, script, or test module, a reviewer cannot independently re-run the live check to confirm the AC is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet: 'a live check exercises rebase:chain + rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance and asserts…'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Tests AC: 'Run with `pytest -m live infra/autolander/tests/test_live_rebase.py`; requires `GERRIT_URL`, `GERRIT_BOT_USER`, and `GERRIT_BOT_PASSWORD` env vars set to the test instance credentials.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit prerequisite declaration (e.g. 'Depends-on: S5') and note in the Tests AC that the live check requires the S5-provisioned SSM secret to be present, or provide a fallback (e.g. skip live check if secret absent) so the unit remains independently runnable as stated.
findings.finding: The live-test AC requires credentials provisioned by S5 ('the bot authenticates as RebarBotNava via its SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5)'), but the Why/sequencing section declares no dependency on S5, and the plan's stated goal is to be 'loadable whole' independently. This is a cross-section contradiction: if S5 has not run, the live check cannot pass its assertion, yet the plan presents this unit as independently completable.
findings.location: AC: Tests (live check) vs. Why section (split out of S2 to keep each unit loadable whole) vs. AC: Gerrit helper (provisioned by S5)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Prerequisites / Dependencies' section: 'Depends-on: S5 (SSM secret for RebarBotNava must exist before the live test can run).' In the Tests AC, add: 'Live check is skipped (xfail/skip) when the SSM secret is absent so the unit remains loadable in isolation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry documenting the architectural decision to keep `infra/autolander/gerrit.py` as a standalone stdlib-only Gerrit client (not importing `src/rebar`), explaining the rationale (deployment/dependency isolation) and the maintenance policy (which calls live here vs. in `src/rebar`).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a second, stdlib-only Gerrit HTTP client (`infra/autolander/gerrit.py`) alongside the existing `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py`, deliberately duplicating the pattern to avoid cross-component imports. This is a novel architectural decision — two parallel Gerrit client implementations in the same repo with different dependency constraints — but no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry is planned to record the rationale, the constraint, or the policy for future maintainers.
findings.location: Files section (`infra/autolander/gerrit.py`) and Gerrit helper AC bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Create `infra/autolander/docs/adr-001-stdlib-gerrit-client.md` (or add a section to an existing `CLAUDE.md`) stating: (1) `gerrit.py` must remain stdlib-only and import-free from `src/rebar` to keep `infra/autolander` independently deployable; (2) it covers only the calls the autolander loop needs; (3) richer Gerrit interactions belong in `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py`. Reference it from both files' module docstrings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define how the autolander prevents concurrent selection+rebase of the same change (e.g., single-instance guarantee enforced by the deployment, an advisory Gerrit hashtag set atomically before rebasing, or loop serialization with a mutex) so no two operations act on the same change simultaneously.
findings.finding: The plan does not address concurrent execution: if two autolander instances run simultaneously (e.g., overlapping loop iterations, or a crash-restart that overlaps with the still-running previous instance), both can select the same front change and both issue rebase:chain, causing a TOCTOU race that can corrupt the patch-set chain or create spurious patchsets.
findings.location: Files: infra/autolander/loop.py; AC: Selection + rebase-routing bullets
findings.suggested_fix: The simplest safe option: document that the loop is strictly single-threaded and single-instance (one pod/process, no overlapping iterations — enforce via a per-iteration lock file or a `asyncio` single-task guarantee). If multi-instance is ever needed, use a Gerrit hashtag (e.g., `autolander:in-progress`) set via the API before issuing rebase and cleared after submit, treating a pre-existing tag as a skip signal.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether infra/autolander/ genuinely cannot import src/rebar (e.g., different container, no pyproject.toml dependency); if the barrier is real, consider extracting ONLY the ~30-line HTTP scaffold (_strip_xssi, GerritError, _request) into a shared, dependency-free module (e.g., infra/autolander/_gerrit_http.py or a common infra/lib/) rather than duplicating the full client.
findings.finding: infra/autolander/gerrit.py duplicates the urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI scaffold already in production at src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py (4+ call sites: voter.py, app.py, reconcile.py, gerrit_client.py) without confirming that the stated packaging barrier ('no import of src/rebar') is real and unavoidable, or evaluating the narrower alternative of extracting only the shared HTTP plumbing into a common stdlib-only module.
findings.location: ## Files — infra/autolander/gerrit.py; ## Acceptance Criteria — 'Gerrit helper' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify and verify the autolander bot's Gerrit HTTP identity: either confirm `RebarBotNava` has an HTTP password provisioned (via a service-user.sh-equivalent step in S5), or rename it to a specific new service account (e.g., `rebar-autolander`) and reference the provisioning script that creates it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the autolander bot 'authenticates as `RebarBotNava`' (a confident identity claim), but repository evidence shows `RebarBotNava` appears only as a Gerrit **Contributors group member** (infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 92), never as a Gerrit HTTP service account with an HTTP password. The actual bot service account with SSM-backed HTTP credentials is `rebar-review-bot` (infra/gerrit/service-user.sh, src/rebar/review_bot/config.py). No service-user.sh invocation, SSM slot name, or account-provisioning script establishes `RebarBotNava` as the autolander's HTTP identity — the claim is unverifiable from the codebase.
findings.location: Gerrit helper AC / Files section: 'the bot authenticates as `RebarBotNava` via its SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5)'
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) add a service-user.sh-style provisioning step in S5 for a new Gerrit service account (e.g., `rebar-autolander`) and reference it by that name in this plan, or (b) if `RebarBotNava` is intentionally the bot identity, explicitly reference the provisioning step (script + SSM slot) that creates its HTTP password — analogous to how `service-user.sh` creates `rebar-review-bot`'s token and stores it at `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the SSM parameter (e.g., `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token`) and its `fetch-secrets.sh` env-var mapping (e.g., `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`) as unenumerated consumers that must be added, or explicitly delegate this to S5 with a concrete parameter name.
findings.finding: The plan states the bot authenticates as `RebarBotNava` via an 'SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5)', but no SSM slot for an autolander bot token exists in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` or `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`. The existing `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token` is mapped exclusively to `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN` for the review-bot (`rebar-review-bot`). Neither an autolander-specific SSM parameter nor its `fetch-secrets.sh` mapping are enumerated as consumers/prerequisites in this plan.
findings.location: AC: Gerrit helper — 'the bot authenticates as `RebarBotNava` via its SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` and `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` as unenumerated consumers (or explicitly note they are S5's responsibility, with a pointer to the relevant S5 child ticket).
findings.finding: `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` is an unenumerated consumer: wiring `infra/autolander/loop.py` into the running service requires adding an autolander service entry (image/build, env vars, volume mounts) to `docker-compose.yml`. Similarly, `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` and `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` would need updating to surface the new credential. None of these files are mentioned in the plan.
findings.location: ## Files section (no mention of docker-compose.yml, fetch-secrets.sh, or compose-up.sh)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Files section: 'Compose wiring (`docker-compose.yml`, `fetch-secrets.sh`) and the SSM token slot are S5 (perpetual-ultrasonic-raccoon) deliverables; S2a only produces the Python modules.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale for choosing full duplication over an intra-infra shared transport module (e.g., why `infra/autolander/_gerrit_http.py` was not preferred) so the approach is explicitly motivated, not just constrained.
findings.finding: The plan's positive rationale for creating a new `infra/autolander/gerrit.py` rather than factoring a shared thin transport layer (e.g., `infra/autolander/_gerrit_http.py`) is absent. The plan states 'no import of src/rebar' as a constraint but never explains why this constraint forecloses an intra-infra shared module, leaving the chosen approach (full duplication of the urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI boilerplate already in src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py) without a stated positive justification.
findings.location: Files section / Gerrit helper AC: 'thin STDLIB-ONLY Gerrit helper (the src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI pattern; no new dependency, no import of src/rebar)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify in the plan why extracting the shared `_request`/`_strip_xssi`/`GerritError` core to a standalone `infra/autolander/gerrit_http.py` (no `src/rebar` import) was rejected in favor of full duplication, or adopt the extraction approach so the two clients share a single, tested HTTP scaffold.
findings.finding: The plan creates `infra/autolander/gerrit.py` as a full parallel reimplementation of the urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI scaffold already in production at `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py`, with the justification 'no new dependency, no import of `src/rebar`'. This rationale, while valid for avoiding a package dependency, does not address the anti-repackaging alternative: extracting the shared HTTP scaffold (the `_request`/`_strip_xssi`/`GerritError` core) into a lightweight standalone module under `infra/` (e.g., `infra/lib/gerrit_http.py`) that both consumers import without any `src/rebar` dependency.
findings.location: Files section: 'infra/autolander/gerrit.py — thin STDLIB-ONLY Gerrit helper (the src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI pattern; no new dependency, no import of src/rebar)'
findings.suggested_fix: Extract `_strip_xssi`, `GerritError`, and `_request` into `infra/autolander/gerrit_http.py`; have both `infra/autolander/gerrit.py` (new) and optionally `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` (or a thin adapter) import from it. This satisfies 'no import of src/rebar' while eliminating duplication.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **Rebase-routing AC:** Specify the exact discriminant field — `RelatedChangesInfo.changes[]` length — and the membership threshold expression (e.g., `len(related['changes']) > 1`) co-located with the chain-detection rule.
findings.finding: The RelatedChanges chain-membership decision rule references the Gerrit response body but never specifies WHICH field constitutes the member count. The AC says 'detect a relation chain via GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related; a >1-member non-merge chain rebases with rebase:chain' — the condition '>1-member' requires an implementer to know that the discriminant is `len(RelatedChangesInfo.changes)`, a fact the plan leaves implied.
findings.location: AC — Rebase-routing bullet: 'detect a relation chain via `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related`; a >1-member non-merge chain rebases with `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain`'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the AC bullet: 'a relation chain is detected when `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related` returns a `changes[]` array with length > 1 (i.e., more than just the change itself); such a non-merge chain uses `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain`.'

findings.finding: The live check AC has no concrete proving command and no stated observable outcome. It says 'a live check exercises rebase:chain + rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance' but specifies neither the invocation (script path, command, required environment) nor the expected evidence of success (HTTP status, resulting patchset number, uploader field value, etc.).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet: 'a live check exercises rebase:chain + rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance'

findings.finding: The rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader AC claims uploader and trailer preservation after rebase, but neither the unit-test step nor the live check step is specified to assert on uploader identity or trailer content post-rebase. The unit tests are described only as covering 'FIFO ordering + merge-vs-chain-vs-single routing' — uploader/trailer preservation is not listed as a unit-test target.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — third bullet: 'RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves the uploader + DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
